ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for plain wheat cracker; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Meiji Plain Cracker is approximately 25 g (about 5 crackers), providing around 110 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Meiji Plain Cracker — Singapore HPB Foodo nutrient record F11080037
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Foodo record F11080037 for Meiji Plain Cracker. Saturated fat not reported by HPB; estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for crackers, standard snack-type (USDA SR Legacy #18228) as the closest approved analogue and documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Crackers, water biscuits (GI 65)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Meiji Plain Cracker in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 65 is assigned based on the closest approved analogous food: plain water biscuit / wheat cracker (GI 65, glucose reference) as published in the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI table. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (65 × 19.4 g) / 100 ≈ 12.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 504 mg/100 g exceeds the 400 mg/100 g high-concern threshold with no meaningful potassium or fibre offset.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Meiji Plain Cracker to no more than 3–4 crackers (~15 g) per occasion to keep sodium intake below 80 mg per serving, and should prefer low-sodium cracker alternatives with sodium below 200 mg/100 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate load of 77.7 g/100 g with only 1.9 g dietary fibre/100 g produces a high glycaemic load (GL ≈ 12.6 per 25 g serving) and rapid postprandial glucose rise.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should restrict Meiji Plain Cracker to a maximum of 2–3 crackers per sitting, always paired with a protein or healthy-fat food (e.g., cheese or nut butter) to blunt the glycaemic response, and should favour wholegrain crackers with fibre above 3 g/100 g as a routine substitute.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (504 mg/100 g) raises blood pressure risk; saturated fat estimated at ~2.5 g/100 g is moderate; refined starch with low fibre contributes to atherogenic dyslipidaemia risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease should treat Meiji Plain Cracker as an occasional snack only, keeping portions to 15–20 g, and should choose wholegrain, low-sodium alternatives to reduce cumulative sodium and refined-carbohydrate burden on cardiovascular risk factors.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load drives glycaemic inflammation; sodium at 504 mg/100 g worsens RA-related tissue swelling; no anti-inflammatory nutrient factors present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid regular consumption of Meiji Plain Cracker due to its pro-inflammatory refined starch and sodium profile, and should replace it with wholegrain crackers topped with anti-inflammatory spreads such as avocado or nut butter.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Meiji Plain Cracker (明治プレーンクラッカー) is a commercially produced, baked wheat cracker manufactured by Meiji, a major Japanese food company. The cracker is light, thin, and crispy with a subtle sweet-salty flavour derived from wheat flour, vegetable oil, salt, and a small amount of sugar. It is an ultra-processed snack food made primarily from refined wheat flour, giving it a high carbohydrate density of 77.7 g/100 g and a low dietary fibre content of 1.9 g/100 g. The energy density is 442 kcal/100 g, with 10.4 g protein and 10.0 g fat per 100 g. Sodium is elevated at 504 mg/100 g, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycaemic index is estimated at 65 (medium GI, analogous to plain water biscuit), with a glycaemic load of approximately 12.6 per 25 g serving, making it unsuitable as a regular snack for people with type 2 diabetes without portion control and protein pairing. Meiji Plain Cracker is widely available in Singapore, Japan, and across Southeast Asia as a snack or accompaniment to dips and spreads. It is vegetarian, vegan, and dairy-free, but contains gluten and is not suitable for those with wheat or gluten intolerance. Clinically, its refined carbohydrate dominance, moderate sodium, and absence of protective fibre, omega-3 fatty acids, or antioxidants make it a low-nutritional-value snack that should be consumed sparingly by individuals with metabolic or inflammatory conditions.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Meiji plain cracker?
Is Meiji cracker high in sodium?
Is Meiji plain cracker suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo record F11080037 (confidence 0.95 per input metadata). Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy #18228 as the closest approved analogue and documented. GI assigned by analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (plain water biscuit, GI 65). All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo source F11080037; this reflects the product formulation where no added sugar is declared and free sugar is below detection threshold — confirmed zero per HPB. calcium_mg is reported as 0.0 and iron_mg is reported as 0.0 per HPB Foodo source F11080037; these are confirmed zero values from the approved source. saturated_fat_g (2.5 g/100 g) is not reported by HPB for this record; value estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry #18228 (Crackers, standard snack-type) as the closest approved analogue — this is an adapted value and should be updated if HPB or manufacturer data becomes available. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific Meiji Plain Cracker product are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on plain water biscuit from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Meiji Plain Cracker was found in any approved source.

improvement_actions_taken: Added Japanese local name (明治プレーンクラッカー) and romanized form to satisfy G6 multilingual requirement for Japanese cuisine.
Added saturated_fat_g (2.5 g/100 g) estimated from USDA SR Legacy #18228 to satisfy Q3 secondary nutrient completeness.
Assigned GI of 65 (analogous estimate, plain water biscuit, University of Sydney GI Database) and calculated GL per serving to satisfy Q4.
Added FoodOn and FoodEx2 ontology anchors to satisfy Q5.
Expanded natural_language_description to exceed 200 characters and cover all Q6 requirements.
Added Cardiovascular Disease health context condition to satisfy Q7.
Renamed diabetes condition key to type2_diabetes with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' per scoring model requirement.
Documented all -1 sentinel values and confirmed-zero values in notes per G3 and g3 rules.
Documented sugar_g confirmed zero justification in notes per zero-macro justification rule.
Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases array per q8 self-check rule.
